name: Hash Table Operations (Open Addressing)
description: A hash table stores key-value pairs by hashing the key to a bucket index; with open addressing, collisions are resolved by probing successive slots until an empty one is found, and when the load factor grows too large the table is resized and all keys are rehashed, keeping average insert and lookup at O(1).
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Key-value pair to store"]
    N2["Compute hash h(key)"]
    N3["Map to bucket index"]
    N4{"Slot occupied?"}
    N5["Probe next slot"]
    N6["Insert at empty slot"]
    N7{"Load factor too high?"}
    N8["Resize + rehash all"]
    N9["Lookup: hash + probe"]
    N10["O(1) average access"]
    N11["Hash table state"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|yes| N5
    N5 -->|next| N4
    N4 -->|no| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 -->|rehash| N2
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
